requests.description: Charlotte is looking for something floral to brighten her new apartment. "I just moved in and everything smells like cardboard boxes! A few drops of something with petals and romance would make it feel like home."
requests.failure_feedback: Charlotte holds the bottle to her nose and sighs wistfully. "It's lovely, darling, but it doesn't make my heart flutter the way flowers do. Could you add more petals next time?"

requests.description: Geneviève wants a gift for her father. "He used to spend every Sunday in his workshop -- the smell of sawdust and cedar takes me right back. Something woody would be perfect, just like I remember."
requests.failure_feedback: Geneviève closes her eyes and shakes her head gently. "It's lovely, but it doesn't quite remind me of what I'm looking for. I need that woody warmth -- the kind that brings old memories flooding back."

requests.description: Noëlle can't resist anything sweet. "I have such a sweet tooth -- I want a perfume that smells like dessert! Something sugary that makes people smile when they walk past me."
requests.failure_feedback: Noëlle giggles and shakes her head. "Oh, it's interesting! But it's not quite sweet enough for me. I was dreaming of something more like vanilla or caramel -- the kind of scent that gives you a sugar rush!"

requests.description: Rémy starts every morning with a jog through the park. "There's nothing like that first breath of green air on a morning run. I'd love something that captures that feeling -- fresh, alive, outdoors."
requests.failure_feedback: Rémy takes a deep breath and frowns. "This is pleasant, but it's not what I smell on the trail, you know? I need something greener, more alive -- like crushed mint underfoot."

requests.description: Pierre needs something efficient to start his day. "My alarm isn't cutting it anymore. I need a scent with bright top notes that hits you right away -- something practical that wakes you up. And make it decent quality, please. I don't like wasting things."
requests.failure_feedback: Pierre examines the bottle matter-of-factly. "It's not bad, but let's be direct: I need a stronger opening. More top notes, better quality. Something that actually does the job."

requests.description: Geneviève is thinking of her grandmother again. "Grand-mère always said the base of a perfume is its soul. I'd like something grounded, with strong base notes -- the kind of scent she would have approved of. Quality matters. She was very particular."
requests.failure_feedback: Geneviève's expression turns wistful. "It's lovely, but it doesn't have the depth I remember. Grand-mère's perfume had such weight to it -- heavier base notes, more soul. This isn't quite there yet."

requests.description: Charlotte is writing love letters and wants perfume for the paper. "I'm dabbing scent on each letter -- something romantic and warm, where the heart of the perfume really sings. Make it good! Love deserves nothing less."
requests.failure_feedback: Charlotte presses the bottle to her heart and shakes her head. "It's pleasant, but the heart isn't passionate enough. I need those rich middle notes to linger on the page -- something that whispers of love."

requests.description: Margot is hosting a garden party and has exacting standards. "I need to smell like I belong among the flower beds and lemon trees. Something floral with a citrus twist -- and it must be genuinely good quality. I won't settle for anything less. My guests will notice."
requests.reward_text: A whispered secret: "They say if you walk the garden path at sunrise, roses and bergamot dance together in perfect harmony."
requests.failure_feedback: Margot wrinkles her nose ever so slightly. "This is close, but I need something more refined. Both the floral and citrus must come through clearly, and the craftsmanship needs to be impeccable. Do try again."

requests.description: Bernard wants to capture a cozy winter memory. "Picture this: a log cabin, snow falling outside, a warm drink in your hands. I want a scent that's sweet and woody at the same time -- that feeling of being safe and warm. It should be well-crafted, from the heart."
requests.reward_text: A whispered secret: "On cold nights, vanilla and sandalwood hold each other in a warm embrace that never fades."
requests.failure_feedback: Bernard gives you a kind smile. "I can almost smell the cabin, but it's not quite warm enough yet. It needs both sweet and woody working together, crafted with care. I know you'll get it -- take your time."

requests.description: Rémy wants to bottle the feeling of an evening hike. "There's something magical about walking through the woods at dusk -- green leaves overhead, the woody path underfoot. Capture that feeling for me, and do it with real skill. Nature deserves the best."
requests.reward_text: A whispered secret: "When the morning dew settles, peppermint and bergamot greet each other like old friends."
requests.failure_feedback: Rémy shakes his head like a trail guide turning you back. "You're close to that twilight feeling, but I need both woody and green working together. And the quality has to be up there -- nature doesn't cut corners."

requests.description: Simone is attending the Perfumer's Gala and needs something show-stopping. "This is the most important night of my life, darling! I need a scent built on a discovered accord, aged to absolute perfection, with floral notes and masterful quality. All eyes will be on me -- I demand nothing less than extraordinary!"
requests.failure_feedback: Simone gasps and clutches her pearls. "Darling, no! It's good, but it's not gala-worthy. I need a true accord, proper aging, and quality that would make the judges weep. This is my moment -- don't let me down!"

requests.description: Geneviève's family perfumery is closing after three generations. "I want one final masterpiece to remember it all by -- a woody scent with an accord at its core, aged until it sings. My grandparents built this place with their hands. Spare no effort. This is for them."
requests.failure_feedback: Geneviève holds the bottle with trembling hands. "I can tell you put heart into this, and that means the world. But it's not quite the legacy piece I need. It must be woody with a strong base, built on an accord, properly aged, and reach quality 8. The family name is at stake."

requests.description: Noëlle is a pastry chef and wants a signature scent. "I want something that captures my art -- sweet and complex, built on harmony. Use a discovered accord, let it age properly, and make it a true masterwork. Like the perfect soufflé -- you can't rush greatness!"
requests.failure_feedback: Noëlle tastes the air and shakes her head with a warm smile. "It smells delicious, but my art demands perfection! I need a sweet blend founded on an accord, properly aged, with quality 8 or higher. Like baking -- no shortcuts!"
